name: 7-Day Python Sprint for Agentic AI
description: A 7-day, 3-hours-per-day plan that leverages your existing MERN knowledge. Each day mixes short video/reading chunks, focused hands-on coding, a mini-project that builds on the previous day, and a quick reflection to cement concepts. By the end of Day 7 you will have a runnable Python skeleton for an agent, a clean Git repo, and a clear roadmap for advancing to full-featured agentic AI frameworks.
finalGoal: You will finish the week able to write, run, and version-control Python scripts, manipulate data with pandas, make async API calls, and have a working prototype of an AI-agent that you can iterate on with more advanced libraries later.

dailyTasks.motivation: Set the foundation – you already know programming concepts from MERN, so focus on Python syntax and the REPL.

tasks.title: Video Overview
tasks.description: Watch a 30-minute intro video (Corey Schafer – Python for Beginners or freeCodeCamp – Python Full Course 2023).

tasks.title: Reading
tasks.description: Read the first two chapters of Automate the Boring Stuff with Python (Installation, Python interpreter, basic data types).

tasks.title: Setup
tasks.description: Hands-on: Install Python 3.11 (or latest) + VS Code + the Python extension. Open a terminal and run `python -i` to explore the REPL.

tasks.title: First script
tasks.description: Practice: Write a script `hello.py` that prints “Hello, AI Agent!” and run it.

tasks.title: Reflection
tasks.description: Reflection: Write 3 bullet points on how Python’s syntax differs from JavaScript.

dailyTasks.motivation: Control flow & collections – the building blocks for any AI-agent logic.

tasks.title: Control Flow Video
tasks.description: Watch 20 min on `if/elif/else`, loops, and list comprehensions (Corey Schafer).

tasks.title: Reading
tasks.description: Read Automate the Boring Stuff – Chapter 2 (Strings) & Chapter 3 (Lists, Dictionaries, Tuples).

tasks.title: Practice
tasks.description: Exercise: Solve 5 small problems from exercism.io (python track) or LeetCode Easy – e.g., reverse a string, sum of list, count vowels.

tasks.title: Mini-project
tasks.description: Build a command-line “To-Do” list that stores items in a JSON file. Use `input()`, `if`, `for`, and list/dict ops.

tasks.title: Reflection
tasks.description: Reflection: Note any syntax quirks that felt natural vs. those that felt foreign.

dailyTasks.motivation: Functions, modules & virtual environments – essential for clean AI-agent code.

tasks.title: Functions Video
tasks.description: Watch 15 min on defining functions, default arguments, `*args/**kwargs`.

tasks.title: Reading
tasks.description: Read Python Crash Course – Chapter 8 (Functions) & Chapter 9 (Classes – just a skim).

tasks.title: Practice
tasks.description: Exercise: Implement `factorial`, `fibonacci`, and a higher-order function `apply_twice(func, value)`.

tasks.title: Environment Setup
tasks.description: Create a virtual environment: `python -m venv venv && source venv/bin/activate`. Install `requests` and `pydantic`.

tasks.title: Mini-project
tasks.description: Write a module `weather.py` that fetches current weather from a free API (e.g., OpenWeatherMap) and exposes a function `get_weather(city)`. Keep the API key in a `.env` file and load it with `python-dotenv`.

tasks.title: Reflection
tasks.description: Reflection: Write a short note on why virtual environments matter for AI projects.

dailyTasks.motivation: Data handling – pandas & JSON, the formats most AI agents consume.

tasks.title: Pandas Video
tasks.description: Watch 20 min “Pandas Basics” (DataCamp free intro or Corey Schafer).

tasks.title: Reading
tasks.description: Read the first 3 chapters of Python for Data Analysis (Importing data, Series, DataFrames).

tasks.title: Practice
tasks.description: Exercise: Load a CSV of sample tweets, clean whitespace, filter rows where `lang == 'en'`, and save the result to `clean.csv`.

tasks.title: Mini-project
tasks.description: Write a script `agent_data.py` that reads a JSON knowledge-base, converts it to a DataFrame, and prints the top-5 most common keys.

tasks.title: Reflection
tasks.description: Reflection: Compare pandas DataFrames to JavaScript arrays of objects – note the advantages for AI pipelines.

dailyTasks.motivation: Intro to HTTP & async – how agents talk to LLM APIs.

tasks.title: HTTP Video
tasks.description: Watch 15 min on `requests` (synchronous) and 15 min on `httpx`/`aiohttp` (asynchronous).

tasks.title: Reading
tasks.description: Read OpenAI’s quickstart Python guide (or any free LLM API doc).

tasks.title: Practice
tasks.description: Exercise: Write a synchronous function `call_openai(prompt)` that posts to the API and returns the response text (use a dummy endpoint if you lack a key).

tasks.title: Practice Async
tasks.description: Exercise: Convert the above to async using `httpx.AsyncClient` and `await`.

tasks.title: Mini-project
tasks.description: Build a tiny “agent” script that reads a user query, sends it to the LLM, and prints the answer. Add simple caching (store last 5 prompts/responses in a JSON file).

tasks.title: Reflection
tasks.description: Reflection: List three ways async improves an AI-agent’s responsiveness.

dailyTasks.motivation: Putting it together – a functional agent skeleton.

tasks.title: Design Video
tasks.description: Watch 10 min on basic OOP design for agents (class with `run`, `think`, `act` methods).

tasks.title: Reading
tasks.description: Read the “Agentic AI” section of LangChain docs (focus on the Python quick-start).

tasks.title: Skeleton Code
tasks.description: Skeleton: Create `agent.py` with a class `SimpleAgent` that loads a knowledge-base (CSV/JSON), has a `plan(query)` method that calls the LLM, and an `execute(plan)` stub.

tasks.title: Integration
tasks.description: Integrate components built in previous days – use pandas for data, async HTTP for LLM calls, and environment variables for keys.

tasks.title: Test
tasks.description: Test run: Ask the agent “What are the top 3 JavaScript frameworks?” and verify it returns a sensible answer.

tasks.title: Reflection
tasks.description: Reflection: Write a 150-word description of how each Python feature you learned contributes to an agent pipeline.

dailyTasks.motivation: Polish, version control, and next-step roadmap.

tasks.title: Git Video
tasks.description: Watch 10 min on Git basics for Python projects (branching, .gitignore, requirements.txt).

tasks.title: Version Control
tasks.description: Create a Git repo for the week’s code, commit each day’s work, write a concise README that explains how to run the agent.

tasks.title: Dependency List
tasks.description: Add a `requirements.txt` (`pip freeze > requirements.txt`).

tasks.title: Linting
tasks.description: Run a code-quality check: install `flake8` and fix any style warnings.

tasks.title: Roadmap
tasks.description: Future-planning: Draft a 5-step learning path for deeper AI-agent work (e.g., LangChain, Prompt Engineering, Retrieval-Augmented Generation, Dockerizing, Deploying to AWS/GCP).

tasks.title: Share
tasks.description: Celebration: Write a short blog post or LinkedIn update summarizing “My 7-day Python sprint for AI agents”.

finalChallenges.title: Hybrid Retrieval Agent
finalChallenges.description: Extend `SimpleAgent` to accept a CSV of FAQs and, when queried, first try to answer from the CSV before falling back to the LLM.

finalChallenges.title: Containerize
finalChallenges.description: Dockerize the agent (write a Dockerfile, build an image, run `docker run`).
